So there's the seven phases of the lead, there's 100 visitor tests. So the next thing I want to talk about are the actual building blocks of these funnels. Okay? So you understand these building blocks, it'll make it a lot easier for the next session we're going to start mapping out our sales funnels. And then after lunch when we start playing with click funnels, okay? All right, so this is basically the graphic I made. I remember one day we sat down in our big old whiteboard and we mapped out these different phases. And so obviously I didn't write down the traffic temperature, it's not really blocked for that. So the other six, here's our six phases that we just went through. Now each of these phases, there's only a couple options for each one of them. Okay, I'll show you guys some examples. So for the pre-frame, for a long time people always ask me, well, how do you do pre-frames? How do you pre-frames? I never had a way to kind of show, we do them all the time. Anyway, so the first time I really thought about it, I said, okay, we do pre-frames, what are the ways we do them? And these are the basically one, two, three, four, five, six, seven. So here's seven pre-frames that we use, okay? The very first one, and that's the images in your book. So I'm gonna go over to the Internet to show you guys some examples of these things. All right, so the first one we're gonna talk about is a quiz. So quiz is like, this is like my new exciting thing I figured out in the last couple months. And the rule that I figured out is Glenn Ledwell, they're in our other mastermind. They own a company called Mind Movies. And they had this webinar they were driving traffic to on Facebook. And they're averaging like one and a half percent conversion rate on Facebook. And they couldn't get it to, they were barely breaking on their media spend and how much they were making on the actual webinar. They kept trying thing after thing after thing and nothing was gonna work for them. And so one day they put up this quiz, and I'll, cool, I'm gonna find their quiz for you real quick, I might not be able to. But they put up this quiz, so they add this little quiz to the front of their funnel. So on Facebook, they drive people here on Facebook to this page. It's just a little video basically saying, here's the headline, here's a 30 second quiz that's gonna reveal to you your number one income crushing problem. They drove this quiz, you click down here, take the quiz and it pops up. And then they ask some questions. And the questions don't even matter you guys, it's just getting them engaged in this process. They ask these questions, question, question, question, question, question. And then it gets them to opt in. Now if you just answered 15 bajillion questions, you're gonna opt in this point? Like, all right, find out the results. They opt in, then take some next page where they sell them on the webinar. So adding in this quiz that really does nothing at all, takes everyone to the same, I think they may segment a little bit, but the ones we do, it does nothing. Just take some next page, and all of a sudden they take it. They more than double their conversion. Went from a campaign that was barely profitable to a campaign that was doubling over on the front end, by adding this little quiz. So quizzes I think one of the best, I'm convinced right now, it's probably one of, if not the best pre-frame, especially coming from Facebook style traffic. Because it just, people like taking quizzes, which is weird, because I've never taken a quiz. Anyone here like taking quizzes? Isn't that weird? Yeah, a couple of you like quizzes. I don't, I would never take a quiz. But people do, apparently, and it doubled, more than double their conversion. So here's a couple examples. Here's a landing page we have that just has a little quiz. Okay, this is our landing page. We took people, we took people through for our body evolution test. So he was saying for the camera, when he put up a squeeze page the first time, he was like, all these people are not gonna see my sales letter now. He's right, 70% of them never see the sales letter. I say click funnels, we're building out a whole bunch of really cool quiz templates in there because I want everyone to be able to do it as well. But they're not in there as of today. And this is a really good strategy. When you're looking back here at this part, there's a lot of traffic coming in, you have no idea who they are. And so you ask quiz questions to segment where they're at. So you can find out, you ask questions about like, are you just getting started doing this for a while, blah, blah, blah. And then based on that, you have a different landing page video for each one. So that's definitely something you can do. I'm not doing it with this funnel because this funnel I'm just targeting. I'm only targeting these people. But if I was doing one where it's more generic, I definitely would want to do that. Great question. I think when we do ours, probably a video of me being like, hey, this is Russell, I got this coolest quiz. It's gonna show you how to double your traffic conversion in cells. It takes quiz and it'll show you how to do it. So take the quiz, find out double their traffic conversion in cells. And the way they do that is they register for my webinar where I'm gonna teach them how to double their traffic conversion in cells. That's the slippery slope we're taking through, okay? All right, so quizzes as a pre-frame. Next one, so next one like article, news, blog. You guys know what those sites, what those are. But this is an example. If you look at anyone who's crushing it with banner ads especially, they're almost always driving somebody through a site like this. It looks like a new site. They got, here's a video of Dr. Oz and they're talking about blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah. And they're pre-framing this thing. And the bottom of it like, hey, this product down here actually does it. You click on there, now it takes you to your landing page. Now you have to be careful with these because a lot of people were making these fake news sites and fake things. And FTC came down and slapped them really, really hard. The biggest key to any kind of marketing guys is don't be an unethical piece of garbage, like tell the truth and it's really weird like you don't get in trouble. You start lying, you do. So don't do what the people got in trouble and make fake news sites. But you can set up a blog, like this one's health lifestyles. Here's a health lifestyle blog. Very tops is advertisement. And this is an article. It's got a video clip from Dr. Oz talking about, which I wouldn't recommend you, he's going after people right now. But conceptually, you see how it works. It's like a news site or an article or a blog doing that. And I make money stuff. What I did one time that works really, really well, I had one of our students, Justin Brooke, you guys know him. I was like, hey man, can you write an article about your experience with me? And then at the bottom, I'm gonna put a link back to my product. He's like, yeah, sure. So here's an article in his blog about me and our experience with me and put a link back to my free plus shipping product. And then we drove traffic directly to his blog. And now what is it? Third party testimony, someone's saying, I'll let some cool, they come click over here and they buy my thing. They didn't know that I engineered that and made it work, but it worked great. So somebody else's blog is a great pre-frame, especially advertising on Facebook, like I can use Justin's hymns and add like a bunch of stuff you can do. So look at that as another pre-frame, news sites, blogs. Video works really good as well. We've had a lot of success to drive people straight to YouTube page. Like hey, check out this video on YouTube. They go to the YouTube page and watch this video and there's a call to action and pushing back to our thing. It's a pre-frame. It's this thing in front of it that gets them to go check out the other thing. Emails, I consider email a pre-frame. If I buy a solo ad and it's sending email out, that is a pre-frame. That's the thing. It comes back, it sends back from the days of direct mail. So back in the day before email and the internet and all this kind of stuff, what would happen is I would go to somebody who had a product and I'd be like, hey, or somebody had a list. And I'd be like, hey, we send my sales letter to your list. And the person would be like, sure. And I'd be like, hey, what would be really cool is if you put a lift letter on the front of it? And lift letter is a one page letter that says, hey, this is Russell. My buddy Joe just showed me this really cool offer. I checked it out. It's awesome. I recommend it. You should check it out from Russell. And they put this lift letter on top of your sales letter and they'd ship out that package. By adding that lift letter, it would increase response two or three times over. It's the same thing with an email. Email is just a lift letter you're sending out to your audience saying, hey, Russell's got this really cool thing. Check it out. They click there and go to the thing. So email, lift letter, whatever you want to call it is a great pre-frame. And then a pre-sell page. This is one of the coolest pre-sell pages. And you guys know Andre Chaperone? He does these pre-sell pages, like the coolest thing in the world. So he has something he wants to sell. So he goes and writes this page. And we're making templates and click funnels for this. They're not in there yet, but they're cool. So this is an example of one called Zero to Hero. And he's telling a story about it. Newbie went from Zero to Hero in seven weeks. It's just like him telling a story. It's an article. It shows pictures. It's kind of like a sales letter, but it's not. It's like an article. And he just tells you some stuff. And then at the end, he pushes you to go here to the actual page where he sells your stuff. So this is a pre-sell little page that he does. He does this as an affiliate. He does this as something he writes a little pre-sell page like that, telling the story, showing a bunch of stuff. He drives you with a pre-sell page, which then takes him to the actual sales page. It's a really cool strategy. A lot of times, you send something directly as an affiliate, send it to somebody else's offer. The page might not convert well. So you add a pre-sell page and you can double, triple how much money you're going to make on an affiliate campaign. So pre-sell pages work awesome. Okay, any questions about pre-frame pages, that first category? Awesome. Second step now is we have to qualify subscribers. All right, the most simple way is a squeeze page. We've got like 40 squeeze page templates like ClickFones. But this is basically what a squeeze page is. I'm sure most of you guys know what it is. But for anyone who doesn't, squeeze pages come from back in the day. You remember like 10 years ago, you go on the internet and you go to somebody's website and these really annoying things would pop up in your face. Like congratulations, you want to blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, give us your email address for, and so you put your email address in to win the free thing and then you'd be out of that person's list, right? And so everyone's making insane amounts of money with these pop-ups, right? They put pop-ups on the site, drive tons of traffic and just start building these lists of hundreds of thousands of people really, really fast. But unfortunately pop-ups drove everybody crazy. And so Google and everyone started putting pop-up blockers everywhere and pop-ups stopped working. And so all these marketers were freaking out like, how are we gonna get email addresses? Like that's what we're making our money, we need email addresses. And so someone, I've heard like five people claim they invented the squeeze page but I think it was Dean Jackson. He's like the most reliable source I think of the group. But he was like, hey guys, what if we took that good old pop-up that made us all that money, instead of having it, here's your website and the thing pops up, what if we took that pop-up and made that the first page? And we sent people to that page, they had the headline and they get their email address. And then from there we show them our actual sales message. People are like, you're stupid, why would you do that? No one's gonna ever see this page. It's like, I think it'll work, I think it'll work. So we put it up there, tested it out, and sure enough, when all said done, what typically would happen, let's say you get 30% of people to show up here. Or 30% of people will give you the email address. Only 30% ever hit this page, 70% don't. But now you got them on your list, and we talked yesterday about the power of a list. Now you can follow up with them, you can try to sell them this thing over and over again. I read one time that it takes an average person seven times getting to know you or experiences or touches with you before they feel comfortable enough to buy. So a lot of people would buy the first time, but a lot of them won't. So you follow up three or four or five times, your conversion, let's say you have a 1% conversion on this page, it'll change to a 2%, and a 3%, and a 5% just by having a really good sequence. And again, we'll talk about tomorrow. But the key here is these squeeze pages give you the ability to do that. So this is an example of a squeeze page, a very, very, very simple one. And I'm gonna show you guys click files after lunch, you'll see like 40 or 50 really high converting ones you can use just with the click of a button. Yeah, so there are some circumstances where I don't want an email, where I don't do a squeeze page or something like that, but they're rare and far between. I would say it's a standard rule to just do one. So we have squeeze page, it is qualified subscribers. You guys are all familiar with popups, they're not nearly like what they used to be, but there's new software that you can get through them. So there's still our popups that work. A squeeze pop, so this is something we added and click funnels right now, it's pretty cool. Is a squeeze pop is basically, you can take this little line of code and put it on your website, it's just a button. It says, hey, click here to get my free newsletter or whatever. And they click on that and it pops up and then somebody can go and subscribe. So we call this squeeze pops. Free plus shipping. Here's an example of a free, let's see. Here's our free plus shipping page for our Skiddy Bean diet. You know it looks just like the Dotcom Seekers Labs one for anyone who's done that. So there's our page. They click here and step one asks for their shipping address. We get their email address, shipping and step two, we get their actual credit card. So everything feels like step one, we're added to our list. So we use that as a tool to get people to join our list. Webinar registrations, this is an example of a webinar registration page. This is our Dotcom Seekers local one. They come here, they pick, it's almost like a little quiz. They pick which day they wanna register, they put their email address in and boom, now they're on my list, right? They qualify themselves as a subscriber. Free account, I was checking out Shopper approved yesterday. So that's these guys' company over here. So this is what they do to get subscribers. You come here and like, hey, get a free account. So I come down here to the bottom, I create my free account, guess what they get? Boom, they got my email address. Now I'm on a list. Now they can follow me, they can close me. So if you got any kind of software or something you can give away free account, boom, now you got them on your list. Okay? And then exit pops, I'm just leaving your site, you have a pop up and also grab this stuff. So when I'm trying to qualify subscribers, those are the one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, seven different ways. I'm trying to get people my list. Okay, so I'm creating an offer, I'm looking at which one of these seven things should I do? If this offered, I wanna squeeze page, yes, or no, I don't wanna squeeze page. I wanna do a webinar registration, right? How about we give them a free account, and then from there we'll do the next thing. So I'm gonna do a free account. So I pick one of these, one of these seven building blocks. Okay, do you guys can see I'm going through this like the way, when I create any offers, what we do is sit down. We look at the six steps here, and pre-frame, which one I wanna do? Okay, let's do a quiz for this pre-frame. Let's do free plus shipping, two-step, we might qualify subscribers. Okay, step three now, how am I gonna qualify my buyers? I go through here and I'm picking one of these building blocks for each step. Okay? So all right, so it's number three, qualifying buyers. The easiest way, or my favorite way, as you guys all know now, is free plus shipping stuff. Here is Ryan and Perry's free credit card offer. Just look how simple it is, free credit card knife. Like, that's like the easiest pitch ever. They charge, let's see. So 295 shipping handling, click here, your credit card, boom, they just qualified a buyer. Okay, so free plus shipping is good. Trial stuff is good, so .comsecretsx. This is one of our programs we've made insane amounts of money on. We used to do dollar trial. Give us a dollar, get a free 30 days to our membership site. Easy way, low barrier of entry to get somebody in. So trials are a great way to qualify buyers. Tripwires, that's what Ryan and Perry call these tripwires. I call them free plus shippings. Tripwires are also, they call, they do a lot of like $7 reports. It's like, hey, buy this report on whatever, pay $7, you can get it. So I consider tripwire like a really, really low ticket product. I don't like those as much as free plus shipping for all the reasons I told you guys earlier, but there's that. Self-liquidating offer is something that's usually more expensive, like a $37 product. And then straight sales, just more expensive, like $100, I'm trying to sell that. So I'm trying to figure out how I'm gonna qualify my buyers, it's usually one of those. Okay, now in most cases, for me, I'm gonna do free plus shipping. That's my favorite, but not all cases. Like when we did our neuropathy supplement, we did free plus shipping for a while, but we couldn't get the numbers quite to work out on it. So we shifted it to a straight sale, and that's what made it all work for me on a straight sale. So sometimes when you're doing your funnel, you're testing it out, you try one thing and it doesn't work. You know what I scrapped the whole thing, it's like, okay, well, for this market or this traffic, whatever reason the free plus shipping didn't work, so let's try a tripwire this time. Well, let's try this, and you can just shift out the piece that doesn't work. Okay, if you look at the body evolution offer that I showed you guys the other day, I'm not sure I can find that paper. Remember I was showing you like, we have 38% opt in here, 0.76% da-da-da-da. The 0.76% we were going with a self-liquidating offer. We were selling the $47 product and was not converting. So we decided to shift it to a free plus shipping and we'll find out, but I think the new version will convert a lot better. So I don't have to redo the whole funnel, just shifting which building block I use. Sometimes we'll fix or change or alter the offer. That's my qualifying buyers. Now we gotta find out my buyers and heat. So after they bought something, I need to upsell them something else, right? So this is an example, I thought this page was really cool. This is Ryan Perry's upsell page for one of their offers. So there's a video like, wait, before you move on, there's another offer. So this is something that just bought my free plus shipping thing, then they come to a page like this, it was a video, and then there's an ad to cart, here's the buy the next thing, the more expensive thing. So that's an example of that. We got a whole bunch of pages like that inside of ClickFunnels as well. So there's one time offer pages, then after we buy that, usually if they say no to that, then we'll have a down sell. Sometimes we'll refer other people's products, and then we have bumps, which is just a lot of times things where we'll increase the price of something right on the order button. Like an example of that. Let's make sure an example of a bump. So dot com secrets, this is an example of a bump. So I hear a master for their credit card info, and then right here, you see I ask really quick like, hey, for an extra $37 you want this? That's what I call a bump. In the order process, I'm like, hey, do you want this really quick? Amazon used to do it all the time, like, hey, you bought this, you want to buy this too? I call those bumps. So that's how I identify my buyers and heat is through one of those four processes, upsells, or one time offers, downsells, recommends somebody else's product, or I do bumps in the process. Those are the four ways I do that. And typically you can do, and I'll show you guys this in the next session, but we can do multiple upsells and downsells, so it doesn't have to be just one. It's always one of those four things. Now after we've got them in the process and we've identified the buyers and heat, we finished our first day, we both went home. Now it's time for me to recording relationship, I'm gonna age and ascend the relationship, so I'm gonna do that through a bunch of different things. Through product launches, through a webinar, through automated webinar, through tele-seminars, a whole bunch of different ways to do that. And then the last step when I'm trying to change the environment, for me, we go to an application page where they apply it to come work with me. They then apply, and I think most of you guys probably fill out form, I'm guessing. There's application form to come in and come and work with us. So that's changing your phone sales, seminars, face-to-face selling. So those are kind of the building blocks we use for each of those six steps. That help you guys kind of see how we do our process? So when you're building your stuff out, like I said, what I do is I'm always looking at this, okay, for this new offer we're creating, what's the pre-frame? Okay, I'm gonna do a quiz, cuz Russell, that's awesome. I think they're cool. Second step, how many qualifiers subscribers? I'm gonna do a squeeze page, or let's do whatever. Third is I'm qualifying buyers, I'm gonna do free plus shipping. Fourth, I'm gonna do an upsell, so I'm gonna do a one-time offer. Fifth, I'm gonna do, after we've got them, then let's put them onto a webinar. We're gonna sell them my $2,000 product. And after the webinar is over, let's get them to apply it on a phone call, we'll sell them our $15,000 thing. Okay, that's literally what I do, you guys. If you were to pay me a ton of money, cut my office, and we sat down on a front-of-the-white board, that's the process we go through when we map out, okay, this is gonna be your front end, this is gonna be your back end, you're up-seller, you're down, so we map the whole thing out, you'd have that, and then we go build it, okay? So what we're gonna do next, we're gonna take a break right now. When we come back, I'm gonna show you guys our nine core funnels, okay?
